title: Install advisor — Reporter shipping metrics to Datadog
description: Reporter DaemonSet with Datadog as the sole TenXSummary metrics destination. Compared with install-reporter-log10x-saas, this exercises the backend_credentials path: any non-`log10x` backend requires a Kubernetes Secret name, which the wizard asks for as Q4. wizard_answers carries the secret name so deterministic mode can satisfy that question the same way Sonnet would in autonomous. Canonical chain: discover_env (snapshot k8s) → advise_install (app=reporter, backends=['datadog'], backend_credentials.datadog.secretName='datadog-credentials'). Test enforces (1) discover_env emits a snapshot id, (2) advise_install carries snapshot_id + app=reporter + backends including 'datadog', (3) advise_install reaches a structurally valid wizard outcome.
prompt: Install the Log10x Reporter on this cluster. Ship the engine metrics to our Datadog account — the DD-API-KEY and DD-APP-KEY live in a Kubernetes Secret named `datadog-credentials` in the cluster.

initial_tool.reason: Install chains start at discover_env. skip_aws=true keeps the eval-harness probe local-only on the demo env.

ground_truth.description: advise_install was called with a snapshot_id arg (chain handoff)
advise_install was called with app='reporter' (prompt names Reporter)
advise_install was called with backends containing 'datadog' (prompt names Datadog as the sole metrics destination)
advise_install reached a structurally valid wizard outcome — plan envelope or a typed wizard mode
